title: Implement MiscodedDistributionCandidate model (models/distributions.py)
description: Frozen Pydantic model: txn_id, date, transaction_type, payee, memo, account, amount (Decimal), reason_codes (list[str]), confidence (High|Medium|Low), score (int >= 0). Per PLAN.md §4.3.

title: Implement equity account detection for distributions
description: Primary detection: QBO equity accounts matching 'distribution'/'draw'/'shareholder'. Query via SessionDataStore. Sum transactions for distribution_total. Per PLAN.md E10.

description: Test valid construction. Test score >= 0. Test confidence levels. Test reason_codes list. Test frozen immutability.

title: Unit tests: miscoding rules (each individually)
description: Test R1: owner payee + expense -> +3. Test R2: memo keyword + expense -> +2. Test R3: transfer + high amount -> +2. Test R4: round number + owner -> +1. Test R5: flagged + high amount -> +1. Test each rule in isolation.

title: Unit tests: combined miscoding scoring
description: Test multiple rules firing on same transaction. Test score accumulation. Test maximum possible score. Test minimum flagging threshold. Test transactions that trigger no rules.

title: Unit tests: confidence level classification
description: Test High confidence threshold. Test Medium threshold. Test Low threshold. Test below minimum (not flagged). Test boundary scores.

title: Unit tests: equity account detection
description: Test 'distribution' keyword match. Test 'draw' match. Test 'shareholder' match. Test non-equity accounts excluded. Test case-insensitive matching.

title: Integration tests: distributions pipeline
description: Mock QBO equity accounts + GL transactions via respx. Full pipeline: detect -> scan -> score -> CSV output. Verify miscoded CSV content. Per PLAN.md §6.2.

title: Implement unified MiscodeDetector engine (reconciliation/miscode_detector.py)
description: Single engine called by both E10 (Distributions) and E12 (Retained Earnings). scan(gl_rows, owner_keywords) returns list[MiscodedDistributionCandidate]. Applies all 5 rules. Single source of truth. Per PLAN.md E10.

title: Snapshot tests: distributions CSV golden file
description: Golden file for distributions summary CSV and likely_miscoded CSV. Column order matches schema. txn_id included in miscoded CSV.

title: Implement miscoding Rule R1: Owner payee + expense account
description: Owner/shareholder payee matched by owner_keywords + expense account = +3 points. Signal: Owner paid from non-equity account. Per PLAN.md E10 rule table.

title: Implement miscoding Rule R2: Memo keyword match on expense
description: Memo contains 'distribution', 'owner draw', 'reimbursement', 'personal', 'transfer' AND account is expense = +2 points. Signal: Text suggests equity treatment. Per PLAN.md E10 rule table.

title: Implement miscoding Rule R3: Transfer pattern + high amount
description: Transfer from business bank to non-equity account AND amount > MISCODE_HIGH_AMOUNT_THRESHOLD = +2 points. Signal: Structural pattern of owner draw. Per PLAN.md E10 rule table.

title: Implement miscoding Rule R4: Round number + owner payee
description: Amount is multiple of MISCODE_ROUND_NUMBER_DIVISOR AND owner payee (matched by keywords) = +1 point. Signal: Heuristic for informal draws. Per PLAN.md E10 rule table.

title: Implement miscoding Rule R5: High amount signal
description: Any flagged transaction (already scored > 0) AND amount > MISCODE_HIGH_AMOUNT_THRESHOLD = +1 point. Signal: Size amplifier. Per PLAN.md E10 rule table.

title: Implement confidence scoring (High/Medium/Low)
description: Confidence: High (score >= MISCODE_CONFIDENCE_HIGH), Medium (score >= MISCODE_CONFIDENCE_MEDIUM), Low (score >= MISCODE_CONFIDENCE_LOW). Below minimum: not flagged. Per PLAN.md E10.

title: Implement owner keyword collection
description: Owner keywords collected once per session. Interactive: prompt user for comma-separated keywords. Non-interactive: --owner-keywords flag (comma-separated). Used by miscoding detection and equity account scanning. Per PLAN.md E10.

title: E13: Payroll Reconciliation (Gusto vs QBO)
description: Gusto total vs QBO payroll account total. Variance calculation with PAYROLL_RECON_TOLERANCE. Auto-skipped if Gusto absent. Simple one-table PDF + CSV output.

title: Implement payroll reconciliation logic (reconciliation/payroll_recon.py)
description: gusto_total = gross_wages + employer_payroll_taxes + employer_401k_contributions. qbo_total = sum of QBO Expense/COGS accounts matching Payroll|Salary|Wages|Employer Tax|401(k). variance = qbo_total - gusto_total. status = RECONCILED if abs(variance) <= PAYROLL_RECON_TOLERANCE else MISMATCH. Per PLAN.md E8.

description: Mock Gusto + QBO data via respx. Full pipeline: fetch both totals -> calculate -> compare -> verify output. Test with reconciled and mismatched data. Per PLAN.md §6.2.

description: Sum from Gusto data: gross_wages + employer_payroll_taxes + employer_401k_contributions. Same aggregation as E9 but total only. Via SessionDataStore (reuse cached payroll data). Per PLAN.md E8.

title: Implement QBO payroll account detection + total
description: Scan QBO accounts: type = Expense or COGS, name matches 'Payroll'|'Salary'|'Wages'|'Employer Tax'|'401(k)'. Sum balances for qbo_total. Via SessionDataStore. Per PLAN.md E8.

description: key='payroll_recon', folder='10_Payroll_Reconciliation', required=True, requires_gusto=True. dependencies: ['payroll_summary']. Auto-skipped if Gusto absent. Simple one-table output. Per PLAN.md E8.

title: Handle payroll recon edge cases
description: QBO has manual payroll journals -> inflates QBO total -> expected mismatch. Gusto sync lag -> minor variance. No matching QBO accounts -> variance = full Gusto amount -> obvious mismatch warning. Per PLAN.md E8.

title: Implement cache key definitions (data/keys.py)

notes: Adding integration-style cache-hit test with respx/httpx

title: Implement SessionDataStore: in-memory cache (data/store.py)
description: Dict-based in-memory cache keyed by cache key. Thread-safe. Returns cached response on subsequent requests within same session. Single instance shared across all deliverable generators during build. Per PLAN.md ADR-7.

title: Implement SessionDataStore: disk cache persistence
description: Write to _meta/private/cache/{cache_key}.json.gz (gzip compressed) + {cache_key}.meta.json (metadata). Read from disk on session start for warm cache. TTL-based expiry after CACHE_TTL_HOURS. Per PLAN.md Appendix E.

title: Implement SessionDataStore: request coalescing
description: Concurrent requests for same cache key must await a single in-flight HTTP call. Lock per cache key protects both read/validate and in-flight request coalescing. Thread-safe. Per PLAN.md Appendix E.

title: Implement SessionDataStore: integrity validation
description: Validate payload_sha256 against uncompressed payload on cache read. On mismatch: treat as miss, quarantine corrupt entry under _meta/private/cache/quarantine/, re-fetch. Per PLAN.md Appendix E.

title: Implement data providers high-level accessors (data/providers.py)
description: Typed query functions: get_pnl(year, method), get_balance_sheet(year, as_of), get_general_ledger(year, month), get_payroll_runs(year), get_accounts(), get_company_info(). Cache keys generated here only (ADR-12a). Deliverables cannot construct endpoint URLs. Per PLAN.md ADR-12a.

title: Unit tests: SessionDataStore cache operations

title: Implement build error handling (continue on failure)
